articles.title: JJ Redick Praises Lakers’ Grit in Game 3 Win
articles.original_title: JJ Redick was impressed at how hard the Lakers played in Game 3
articles.summary: Silver Screen and Roll relays former player JJ Redick’s comments after the Lakers’ hard‑fought victory over the Rockets in Game 3. Redick highlighted the team’s physical effort despite missing stars like Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves, and noted the low‑scoring, grind‑type nature of the series.
key_points: Lakers played without key contributors Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves.
The game was marked by defensive intensity and low shooting percentages.
Redick commended the team’s toughness and execution in a physically demanding series.

articles.title: Joel Embiid Cleared to Return for Game 4 vs. Celtics
articles.original_title: 76ers star Joel Embiid cleared to return in Game 4 vs. Celtics weeks after emergency appendectomy
articles.summary: Philadelphia 76ers star center Joel Embiid has been medically cleared to play in Game 4 against the Boston Celtics, just weeks after undergoing an emergency appendectomy. The recovery timeline suggests he missed the first three games of the series but will be available to help the Sixers attempt a comeback.
key_points: Embiid underwent emergency appendix surgery earlier in the month.
Doctors have cleared him for Game 4, ending his three‑game absence.
His return comes as the Sixers look to stay competitive in the series.

articles.title: Barnes, Ingram Lead Raptors to 93‑89 Win Over Cavaliers, Series Tied 2‑2
articles.original_title: Barnes, Ingram score 23 each as Raptors beat Cavaliers 93-89 to tie series at 2-2
articles.summary: Scottie Barnes hit the decisive free throws in the final minute and finished with 23 points as the Toronto Raptors defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 93‑89, tying the first‑round series at two games each. Brandon Ingram also contributed 23 points for Toronto.
key_points: Barnes scored 23 points and made the go‑ahead free throws in the closing minute.
Ingram matched Barnes with 23 points for the Raptors.
The victory levels the Eastern Conference first‑round series at 2‑2.

key_points: Kerr cites the presence of LeBron James and Kevin Durant as justification for limiting Tatum's minutes.
The decision was based on roster balance and game‑plan considerations.
